title: Thelypteris sakayensis
taxonomy: Thelypteris sakayensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen ferns native to tropical regions. The family Thelypteridaceae includes over 1,000 species of ferns, mostly found in the tropical and subtropical parts of the world. Thelypteris sakayensis is native to the Philippines, specifically in the Mindanao region. This species is typically found growing in shaded areas with high humidity and moderate temperatures.
uses: Thelypteris sakayensis is a species of fern native to the Philippines. However, information on its specific medicinal uses is limited. Thelypteris sakayensis may have potential health benefits, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in treating various ailments.
